message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>If a health care workers (HCW) experiences COVID-19 associated symptoms, SARS-CoV-2 testing must be performed as soon as possible, to prevent transmission of the virus and to guarantee continuity of care. The gold standard for the detection of SARS-CoV-2, RT-PCR, has a high sensitivity but usually takes 6-8 hours. Lateral flow antigen assays take only 15-30 minutes and do not need any high tech equipment.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>In a prospective study of our hospital’s HCWs, we evaluated the sensitivity of the Panbio™ COVID-19 Ag Rapid Test (Abbott) against the molecular test Aptima™ SARS-CoV-2 Assay (Hologic) which uses Transcription Mediated Amplification (TMA). TMA positive samples were further subjected to a quantitative real-time SARS-CoV-2 PCR to obtain Ct values as an indication of the viral load.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>Of 1101 HCWs included in the study between November 2020 and February 2021, 84 (7.6%) were TMA positive, of which 48 (57.1%) were antigen test positive. Most false negative antigen test results occurred if the duration of symptoms had been ≤1 day or ≥7 days. Sensitivities for symptom onset of ≤1, 2 or 3 days were 47.1%, 63.0% and 66.7% respectively.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>The Panbio™ rapid test is fast and easy to perform, but is not a suitable SARS-CoV-2 test to confirm or exclude COVID-19 in HCWs with a very recent onset of symptoms.</jats:p>
title:
Evaluation of the Panbio
                  <sup>™</sup>
                  rapid antigen test for COVID-19 diagnosis in symptomatic health care workers

reference.unstructured: Ritchie, H. et al.. Coronavirus (COVID-19) Hospitalization. OurWorldInData.org https://ourworldindata.org/covid-hospitalizations (2021).
Coronavirus disease (COVID-19) situation reports. World Health Organization www.who.int/emergencies/diseases/novel-coronavirus-2019/situation-reports (2021).

Antigen-detection in the diagnosis of SARS-CoV-2 infection using rapid immunoassays. Interim guidance. World Health Organization https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/antigen-detection-in-the-diagnosis-of-sars-cov-2infection-using-rapid-immunoassays (2021).
